spec_match.rationale: Touches outerWidth, outerHeight, top-left from the chromestatus summary but skips screenLeft, screenTop — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, canvas, eventListener); controls include "Open snapped popup"; "Open centred popup"; "Refresh values" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Only the happy path is shown — no error handling, feature detection, or boundary inputs are surfaced.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5164958878531584 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on popup-placement
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Show true window position on Android introduces screenLeft, screenTop but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://www.w3.org/TR/cssom-view-1/#dom-window-screenx

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on popup-placement
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: popup-placement covers the happy path of Show true window position on Android and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
